structures.name: Mini-Movie Method
structures.description: Splits the novel into sequential “mini-movies”—each with its own arc and hook—so long-form stories stay vivid and propulsive. Strong for planners who think in cinematic sequences and chapter-sized units.
structures.author: Carol Hughes and Linda Workman Smith (novel-planning adaptation of sequence thinking).

phases.title: Opening Image
phases.description: A snapshot of the world before change; establish tone and the starting emotional baseline.

phases.title: Trigger
phases.description: The event that kicks off this mini-arc and forces a response.

phases.title: Reaction
phases.description: Immediate fallout: fear, denial, or action that reveals stakes and character.

phases.title: New Direction
phases.description: A choice or pivot that commits the character to a fresh path.

phases.title: Midpoint Shift
phases.description: A reversal or revelation that raises stakes and changes strategy or understanding.

phases.title: Complications
phases.description: Pressure tightens as obstacles multiply and old tactics stop working.

phases.title: Dark Moment
phases.description: The low point where success feels impossible or the cost feels unbearable.

phases.title: Final Decision
phases.description: The decisive commitment that launches the climax—no more hedging.

phases.title: Climax
phases.description: The payoff beat: confrontation, revelation, or release that resolves the mini-arc’s conflict.

phases.title: Closing Image
phases.description: Mirror or contrast the opening to show what changed in world, relationship, or self.
